blocks.content: Sometimes a program needs not only to show information, but also to receive data from the user.

For example, a program may ask the user to enter:

• a name
• an age
• a login
• or other information.
In console programs we use the command `Console.ReadLine` to read data from the console.
`Console.ReadLine()` reads the text that the user enters in the console.

Important to remember:

`Console.ReadLine()` always returns text.

Because of this, the result should be stored in a variable of type `string`.
Let's look at the first line of code.

`string name = Console.ReadLine();`

`Console.ReadLine()` reads the text from the console.

The entered text is stored in the variable `name`.
`string` means that the variable stores text.
Now look at the second line.

`Console.WriteLine(name);` prints the value stored in the variable `name`.

So the program shows the same text that was entered.
Retype this code and check it.

Read text from the console and store it in a variable named `city`.

Then print this variable using `Console.WriteLine`.

`Console.ReadLine()` always reads text from the console.

Because of this, the value should be stored in a variable of type `string`.
